name: Green Tara
openingText:
དུས་གསུམ་རྒྱལ་བ་སྲས་བཅས་སྐྱེད་པའི་ཡུམ།
Dü sum gyal wa sé ché kye pé yum
Mother of the Buddhas of the three times and their heirs

བཅོམ་ལྡན་རྗེ་བཙུན་འཕགས་མ་སྒྲོལ་མ་དང་།
Chom den je tsün pak ma drol ma dang
The victorious one, foremost and exalted Tārā

དབྱེར་མེད་བླ་མ་སངས་རྒྱས་ཀུན་འདུས་ལ།
Yer mé la ma sang gye kün dü la
Indivisible from my guru, embodiment of all the Buddhas

སྒོ་གསུམ་རྩེ་གཅིག་གུས་པས་གསོལ་བ་འདེབས།
Go sum tse chik gü pé sol wa deb
With single-pointedness of body, speech and mind, I respectfully supplicate you

འཆི་མེད་རྡོ་རྗེ་བདེ་བའི་ཡེ་ཤེས་ཀྱིས།
Chi mé dor je de wé ye she kyi
With your deathless and blissful vajra wisdom

སྨིན་ཅིང་གྲོལ་ནས་དོན་གཉིས་མྱུར་འགྲུབ་ཤོག།
Min ching drol né dön nyi nyur drub shok
Ripen and liberate me such that twofold benefit is swiftly won
closingText:
དགེ་བ་འདི་ཡིས་མྱུར་དུ་བདག
Gé wa di yi nyur du dag
By this merit, may I swiftly

འཕགས་མ་སྒྲོལ་མ་འགྲུབ་གྱུར་ནས།
Pak ma Drol ma drub gyur né
Attain the state of Noble Tara

འགྲོ་བ་གཅིག་ཀྱང་མ་ལུས་པ།
Dro wa chik kyang ma lü pa
And may every single living being without exception

དེ་དག་ས་ལ་འགོད་པར་ཤོག།
Dé dak sa la gö par shog
Be established in that very same level.
deityInfo:
GREEN TARA - The Mother of All Buddhas

Significance:
Green Tara is the female embodiment of enlightened activity and compassion. She is known as the 'Swift Liberator' because she responds quickly to prayers. Tara means 'Star' or 'She Who Saves'.

Origin Story:
According to legend, Tara was born from a tear of Chenrezig as he looked upon the suffering of beings. His tear formed a lake, and from its waters emerged Tara.

Key Qualities:
• Swift compassion and action
• Protection from fear and danger
• Removal of obstacles
• Active engagement in helping beings
• Courage and fearlessness

The Mantra: OM TARE TUTTARE TURE SOHA
This mantra invokes Tara's three aspects:
• OM - Body, speech, and mind of the Buddhas
• TARE - Liberation from samsara
• TUTTARE - Liberation from the eight fears
• TURE - Liberation from disease
• SOHA - May the meaning take root

Visualization:
Green Tara appears green in color, representing active compassion. Her right hand is extended in the gesture of generosity, holding a blue lotus. Her left hand holds a blue lotus at her heart, fingers in the gesture of refuge. She sits in a relaxed posture with one leg extended, ready to spring into action.

Eight Fears Tara Protects From:
1. Lions (pride)
2. Elephants (ignorance)
3. Fires (anger)
4. Snakes (jealousy)
5. Bandits (wrong views)
6. Prisons (attachment)
7. Floods (desire)
8. Demons (doubt)

Benefits of Practice:
• Overcomes fears and anxieties
• Removes obstacles in daily life
• Provides swift protection
• Increases courage and confidence
• Helps accomplish goals quickly
